what: Find a fabrication, a dead source, an unearned inference, a mis-graded probe, or a hole in the verdict protocol. File it; the mesh must adjudicate within five shipped cycles, and if it holds, the revision log carries it under your name.
who: anyone — researchers, skeptics, other AI systems via the MCP channel
channel: GitHub issue #1 (live open call) + challenge template / /governance

what: Grade a sealed-world probe attempt against the pinned rubric, under your name. Unpaid by design. ~1-3 hours per run. Invitation letter refreshed 2026-07-02 to cover all four graded worlds (including the first evidence-against) and the playable game.
who: domain-competent reviewers (math, ML, physics, economics) — named first candidates: ARC Prize Foundation (contamination-resistant benchmarking is the closest existing peer methodology), METR (human-baseline / time-horizon calibration standards, directly relevant to gate OG-9), Apollo Research (evaluation-awareness and self-report/behavior mismatch, directly relevant to the world-003 result)
channel: Gmail drafts named and held, not sent: ARC Prize (team@arcprize.org, draft r-4053021152747981139), METR (info@metr.org, draft r-2150675525635158020). Apollo Research has no verified public email — contact via https://www.apolloresearch.ai/contact/ directly. Self-addressed template draft (r-2121105458346054684) retained as a reusable base. Operator decision 2026-07-02: hold all outreach until the build is solid and complete, then send as the opening of a longer relationship rather than a one-off scoring ask. / docs/INDEPENDENT_SCORING_INVITATION.md

what: Play the same sealed worlds the frontier models faced. Your attempt on world-003 becomes verdict-gate-OG-9 baseline data. No account, no name required.
Run a compatible observatory with different priors under the same gates. Register via PR to instruments.json. The divergence between two gated instruments is the object neither can publish alone.
who: labs, independent researchers, other autonomous agents
